results.title: My childhood. My ain folk
results.creator_credit: Bill Douglas, Geoffrey Evans, Nick Nascht and British Film Institute
results.brief_description: These first two instalments of the autobiographical “Trilogy” which was to be completed with “My way home” (1978) comprise an achievement unique in British cinema. The events experienced in the films by Jamie closely parallel those lived through by Douglas himself but his approach is impressionistic. The mystification this creates is all of a piece with the films’ effect; communication is a commodity which (like language itself) is in short supply in Jamie’s life. The meaning resides more fully in the visual structure. Tragic as the films are it is the ultimate victory of the imagination over circumstances that “My way home” will subsequently explore.

results.title: My brother Tom
results.creator_credit: Pino Amenta, Rod Hardy and Crawford Productions
results.brief_description: St. Helen, 1938. A town divided by bigotry and charged emotions. World War II is raging in Europe, but all is peaceful in the small town of St. Helen. Days are unaffected by the fighting until a tragedy brings the reality of war to the town’s doorstep, a tragedy that shatters the entire community. At the same time, a love blossoms amidst age-old religious conflict. Adapted from the novel by James Aldridge.

results.title: Cycle
results.creator_credit: Suzanne Gervais, Pierre Heberrt and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: A film of free-hand line animation, where the movement as much as the designs reflects the artist’s thoughts on the life of humans and their universe. What evolves is a stream of ideas about the elemental situation of humankind, poised midway between the primary dust and the measureless universe yet unplumbed. A film without words; titles in French, music by Maurice Blackburn. (Award: San Francisco.)

results.title: Martin Chuzzlewit
results.creator_credit: Pedr James, Chris Parr, BBC and WGBH (Boston)
results.brief_description: Charles Dickens’ classic tale is brought to the screen with an excellent cast of Paul Scofield, John Mills, Pete Postlethwaite, Tom Wilkinson and Julia Sawalha. Martin Chuzzlewit is a very wealthy old man. Having disinherited his grandson Martin, he only trusts his companion, the orphan Mary Graham, employed on the understanding that she will get no money. With a fortune to grasp, Chuzzlewit’s family bring forth all their cunning, greed and selfishness. There is the snivelling hypocrite Pecksniff with his two daughters Mercy and Charity and the evil Jonas who will resort to murder to gain the money.

results.title: Night of the living dead (1968)
results.creator_credit: George A. Romero, Karl Hardman, Russell W Streiner and Image Ten
results.brief_description: A group of escapees from a growing army of flesh-eating zombies spreading across the countryside takes refuge in a house. With EC horror comics of the fifties as an acknowledged influence, this film, at times technically rough, was produced independently on a low budget. It has become a horror/science fiction classic, which, like “The Birds”, can be placed in the Armageddon sub-genre: the world threatened with extinction. It is also one of the earliest horror films in which nihilistic black humour is used to systematically destroy the underpinnings of an ordered society as generic conventions, and the expectations that they arouse, are undercut. This film offered terror to audiences, without the assurance that it was an otherworldly threat - in Romero’s classic the danger lay from within bland suburban confines. The casting of an African-American man as the hero, and the unforgettable ending, is a reflection of the incendiary politics of the times. Cast includes Duane Jones, Judith O’Dea, Karl Hardman, Marilyn Eastman.

results.title: Night of dark shadows
results.brief_description: Adapted from the television series “Dark shadows” this film tells the story of artist Charles Collins and his new wife, and of things that go bump in the night. After moving into his recently inherited family mansion, Collins begins to experience strange dreams - dreams of the past, dreams that suddenly are not dreams any more. Will he follow the same path as his family before? Cast includes David Selby, Monica Rich, Christopher Pennock, Lara Parker, Grayson Hall, Diana Millay.

results.title: Jump jet
results.creator_credit: Derek Smith and British Broadcasting Corporation
results.brief_description: The aeroplane was invented in the early 1900s. Since then there have been only three major breakthroughs in aviation: the jet, the helicopter, and the jump jet. Traces the development of the vertical take-off jet in Britain, with some reference to developments in other countries. Narrated by Richard Maddock.

results.title: Jumanji [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Joe Johnston, Scott Kroopf, William Teitler and Tri-Star Pictures
results.brief_description: For 26 years Adrian Parrish has been trapped inside an ancient board game called Jumanji. The two children who release him also unwittingly unleash a group of wild jungle creatures who go on a rampage. Alan must save his hometown from destruction or risk being exiled back into the game. Cast includes Robin Williams as Adrian Parrish.

results.title: It wasn't easy
results.creator_credit: Nico Crama, Arthur Hammond, National Film Board of Canada and Nederlandse Omroep Stichting
results.brief_description: Half a million people of Dutch origin live in Canada. This film looks at the unique character of the Dutch Canadians, their reasons for leaving the Netherlands and their contribution to Canadian society and culture. A cross section of Dutch Canadians living across Canada are interviewed.

results.title: It takes a lot of help
results.creator_credit: John Peer Nugent and Core Communications Group
results.brief_description: Uses music by the Beatles, with narration by Lorne Greene, to explore the numerous avenues available to groups of citizens who are concerned with preventing drug abuse. Describes various of these avenues available both to the inidividual and communities combating drug abuse.
